column1: How can you update Lambda functions without changing code references everywhere?
column2: Use Lambda aliases - keep existing functions intact, create new versions, and reassign the PROD alias to the new version when ready

column1: What is the maximum amount of memory for a Lambda function?
column2: 10 GB

column1: What is the default timeout value for a Lambda function?
column2: 3 seconds (can be set from 1 second to 900 seconds/15 minutes)

column1: What is the maximum processing time for Lambda functions?
column2: 900 seconds (15 minutes)

column1: How can you increase Lambda CPU performance?
column2: Increase RAM allocation - CPU cannot be increased directly but scales proportionally with memory

column1: What should you do if Lambda processes are timing out due to long execution times?
column2: Check timeout configuration is adequate, increase RAM (which increases CPU), or break process into steps using Step Functions

column1: What programming language is NOT supported by Lambda?
column2: Rust (as of the writing date)

column1: What is the default limit for concurrent Lambda executions?
column2: 1,000 concurrent executions (soft limit that can be raised through AWS Support)

column1: What is the difference between Lambda function policy and execution policy?
column2: Function policy defines which AWS resources can invoke the function; execution policy (IAM role) defines which AWS resources the function can access

column1: How does Lambda handle failed executions for asynchronous invocations?
column2: Lambda automatically retries failed executions and can forward unprocessed payloads to a Dead Letter Queue (SQS or SNS)

column1: What is the best way to manage multiple configuration values for Lambda?
column2: Use Systems Manager Parameter Store with hierarchical values to fetch many configuration items at once

column1: Should you use environment variables for all Lambda configuration?
column2: Environment variables are suitable for non-sensitive values, but centralize configuration administration in Parameter Store

column1: What is the most cost-efficient way to trigger Lambda for processing S3 uploads?
column2: Configure S3 event notifications to trigger Lambda directly when objects are uploaded (more efficient than scheduled polling)

column1: How should you send daily status messages to multiple administrators?
column2: Create an SNS topic, subscribe administrators, and use EventBridge with a daily cron schedule to send messages to the SNS topic

column1: Does SQS support subscriptions for broadcasting messages?
column2: No, SQS queues do not support subscriptions (use SNS for pub-sub)

column1: Can Lambda send outgoing email on port 22?
column2: No, port 22 is blocked on Lambda as an antispam measure (email uses port 25, and port 22 is for SSH)

column1: What happens when Lambda function has access issues with S3?
column2: The function executes but cannot access S3 objects - this indicates an execution policy/IAM role issue, not a function policy issue

column1: Can you use Step Functions to orchestrate long-running Lambda processes?
column2: Yes, breaking processes into steps with Step Functions avoids timeout errors and provides better insight into performance

column1: What are Dead Letter Queues (DLQ) used for in Lambda?
column2: To alert you to processing issues and allow retry of failed executions (can be SQS queue or SNS topic)

column1: Will a DLQ retry solve Lambda timeout issues?
column2: No, if processing time is too long, a retry won't fix it - you need to increase timeout, increase RAM/CPU, or break into smaller steps
